COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Molecular Pathology Laboratory вЂ” Solid Tumor NGS Panel

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Patient Name:         Marlena Okonkwo
Date of Birth:        [on file]
MRN:                  [on file]
Ordering Physician:   Medical Oncology
Diagnosis (Submitted): Metastatic triple-negative breast carcinoma, right iliac wing
Specimen ID:          [LAB-BN-XXXXXX]
Specimen Type:        CT-guided core needle biopsy, right iliac wing (formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded, FFPE)
Date of Procedure:    [DATE]
Date Received:        [DATE]
Date Reported:        [DATE]
Tumor Cellularity (Pathologist Estimate): 45%
Tumor Cellularity (Adequacy):             ADEQUATE for comprehensive genomic profiling

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
CLINICAL INDICATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
39-year-old premenopausal woman with known germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.3750G>A, p.E1250*). Prior diagnosis of stage IIB triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast (ER-/PR-/HER2-negative, grade 3, Ki-67 85%), treated with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense AC x4 cycles followed by weekly paclitaxel x12 weeks. Pathologic response at surgery: ypT1c ypN0(i+), RCB-II. Subsequently treated with adjuvant capecitabine x6 cycles and adjuvant pembrolizumab (17 total cycles). Presented with new right-sided rib pain at 18 months post-surgery; imaging revealed osseous lesions (right 5th rib, T10, right iliac wing) and two hepatic lesions consistent with distant metastatic recurrence. CT-guided biopsy of the right iliac wing performed for pathologic and genomic characterization. Prior primary tumor NGS from core needle biopsy performed at time of diagnosis (results on file for comparison).

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
METHODOLOGY
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Platform: Hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing, 324-gene comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) panel. Analysis includes coding exons of all 324 genes plus selected intronic and intergenic regions for fusion and copy number detection. DNA was extracted from FFPE tissue following macrodissection to enrich for tumor content. Libraries were prepared using standard adaptor ligation. Sequencing performed on Illumina platform with mean exon coverage >500x. Bioinformatic analysis performed using validated somatic variant calling pipelines. Germline vs. somatic status assessed by comparison with matched non-neoplastic (buffy coat) DNA where available; germline variants confirmed by reflexed germline testing. Copy number variants assessed by comparative analysis versus internal reference diploid samples. Microsatellite instability (MSI) status assessed by analysis of >200 microsatellite loci. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) calculated as number of somatic mutations per megabase of sequenced coding genome. Homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) score and genomic loss of heterozygosity (gLOH) calculated using validated computational algorithm.

Limitation: This assay detects alterations in tumor-derived DNA only. Some classes of genomic alterations (e.g., epigenetic silencing, large structural rearrangements outside captured regions) may not be detected. Variants of unknown significance (VUS) not listed here do not exclude their potential future clinical relevance.

SECTION 1: SHORT VARIANTS (SUBSTITUTIONS AND INDELS)

TP53      p.R248W             Missense       45%     Pathogenic         Present in primary (VAF 48%) вЂ” stable
BRCA1     p.E1250*            Nonsense       49%     Pathogenic         Present in primary (VAF 51%) вЂ” germline confirmed, stable
RB1       p.Q670*             Nonsense       38%     Pathogenic         Present in primary (VAF 39%) вЂ” stable
PIK3CA    p.H1047R            Missense       18%     Pathogenic         ACQUIRED вЂ” NOT present on primary tumor NGS

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
* Note: PIK3CA p.H1047R is an activating mutation in the helical domain of PI3K-alpha (encoded by PIK3CA exon 20). This variant was absent on primary tumor sequencing (wild-type at diagnosis) and was subsequently detected at low VAF (1.1%) on concurrent liquid biopsy (ctDNA), consistent with subclonal or treatment-emergent acquisition.

INTERPRETATION AND CLINICAL ACTIONABILITY
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

This metastatic bone biopsy specimen demonstrates a genomic landscape consistent with clonal evolution from the previously profiled primary triple-negative breast carcinoma, with the acquisition of several new somatic alterations in the metastatic setting. The following clinically relevant findings are highlighted:

--- TIER I: STRONG CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE ---

[1] BRCA1 p.E1250* (germline-confirmed, biallelic inactivation via somatic LOH)
This pathogenic germline nonsense variant results in premature truncation of the BRCA1 protein, abrogating its homologous recombination DNA repair function. Biallelic inactivation (confirmed by somatic loss of heterozygosity at the second allele) renders tumor cells dependent on alternative DNA repair pathways. This variant was present in the primary tumor and is retained in the metastasis, confirming its role as a founding driver alteration. This finding is of strong direct therapeutic relevance: BRCA1/2 germline-mutated, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer is a validated indication for PARP inhibitor therapy (olaparib, talazoparib; Tier I evidence, FDA-approved, OlympiAD and EMBRACA trials). Elevated HRD score (61) and high gLOH (28%) further support a robust homologous recombination-deficient tumor phenotype. This finding was the basis for initiation of olaparib 300 mg BID as noted in clinical records.

[2] PIK3CA p.H1047R (ACQUIRED somatic, VAF 18%)
This is an activating missense mutation in exon 20 (helical domain) of PIK3CA, one of the two most common hotspot mutations in PI3K-alpha (alongside p.E545K). It results in constitutive activation of PI3K signaling and downstream AKT/mTOR pathway engagement. This variant was not detected on primary tumor sequencing (performed at diagnosis) and represents a treatment-emergent acquired somatic alteration, consistent with clonal evolution during neoadjuvant and adjuvant systemic therapy. In the context of the co-existing PTEN homozygous deletion, PIK3CA p.H1047R further amplifies PI3K pathway activation. Notably, PIK3CA p.H1047R is a validated actionable target in hormone receptor-positive metastatic breast cancer (alpelisib + fulvestrant; SOLAR-1 trial). Its actionability in the TNBC setting remains investigational; however, this mutation would qualify the patient for PI3K-targeted clinical trial enrollment and may be of future clinical relevance if disease progresses on current PARP inhibitor therapy.

--- TIER II: POTENTIAL CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE ---

[3] CDK4 Amplification (copy number 8, ACQUIRED)
High-level CDK4 amplification is identified as a new finding in the metastatic specimen, absent from the primary tumor. CDK4 amplification co-occurring with CDKN2A homozygous deletion (see below) results in unchecked CDK4/6 kinase activity, bypassing cell cycle checkpoints at the G1/S transition. While CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) are primarily approved in HR+ breast cancer, CDK4 amplification represents a biologically plausible acquired mechanism of resistance to prior therapies and is a potential future therapeutic target. CDK4 amplification in TNBC is an area of active clinical investigation.

[4] CDKN2A Homozygous Deletion (ACQUIRED)
CDKN2A (encoding p16/INK4A and p14/ARF) is homozygously deleted in this metastatic specimen, representing a new alteration not present at primary diagnosis (previously heterozygous point mutation p.P114L). Homozygous loss of CDKN2A eliminates the primary negative regulator of CDK4/6, synergizing with CDK4 amplification to drive unrestrained cell cycle progression. The concurrent acquisition of CDK4 amplification and CDKN2A biallelic loss in the metastatic setting strongly suggests convergent selection for CDK4/6 pathway activation during disease evolution.

[5] PTEN Homozygous Deletion (present in primary, retained in metastasis)
Biallelic PTEN loss results in constitutive PI3K/AKT pathway activation independent of upstream signaling. In the context of acquired PIK3CA p.H1047R in the metastatic clone, PTEN loss further amplifies PI3K-AKT signaling. PTEN loss is additionally associated with reduced responsiveness to immune checkpoint inhibitors and may in part explain the decrease in PD-L1 CPS from 15 (primary tumor) to 8 (metastatic specimen).

[6] MYC High-Level Amplification (copy number 18, increased from 14 in primary)
Progressive increase in MYC amplification between primary and metastatic specimens is consistent with ongoing chromosomal instability and clonal evolution. MYC amplification drives global transcriptional reprogramming and is associated with aggressive disease biology and poor prognosis. MYC is not currently a direct pharmacologic target, though MYC-driven tumors may exhibit specific synthetic lethal dependencies.

--- TIER III: UNCERTAIN/ADDITIONAL SIGNIFICANCE ---

[7] TP53 p.R248W (present in primary, retained in metastasis; VAF stable at 45%)
The R248W mutation is one of the most common gain-of-function TP53 hotspot mutations. It acts through dominant-negative inhibition of wild-type p53 and acquisition of neomorphic oncogenic activities (enhanced invasion, altered gene expression programs). The stable VAF between primary and metastatic specimens is consistent with a clonal founder event. TP53 mutations remain without direct targeted therapy in clinical use.

[8] RB1 p.Q670* (present in primary, retained in metastasis; biallelic inactivation confirmed)
Biallelic RB1 inactivation (truncating mutation + hemizygous deletion of second allele) eliminates a critical tumor suppressor function at the G1/S checkpoint. RB1 loss may contribute to resistance to CDK4/6 inhibitors and immunotherapy. It is also associated with transition to an androgen receptor-expressing or neuroendocrine phenotype in some breast cancers, though this was not evaluated on the present specimen.

COMPARISON WITH PRIMARY TUMOR NGS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Comparison with prior comprehensive genomic profiling of the primary left breast tumor (core needle biopsy, performed at initial diagnosis) reveals the following key changes in the metastatic setting:

  Alterations RETAINED from primary:    TP53 p.R248W, BRCA1 p.E1250*, PTEN homozygous deletion, RB1 p.Q670*, MYC amplification
  Alterations with INCREASED magnitude: MYC amplification (CN 14 в†’ 18), HRD score (54 в†’ 61), gLOH (22% в†’ 28%), TMB (9.2 в†’ 11.4 mut/Mb)
  NEWLY ACQUIRED alterations:           PIK3CA p.H1047R (VAF 18%), CDK4 amplification (CN 8), CDKN2A homozygous deletion
  Alterations LOST or NOT DETECTED:     CDKN2A p.P114L (now superseded by homozygous deletion)

The overall picture is consistent with oligoclonal evolution under treatment selection pressure, with convergent activation of the CDK4/6 and PI3K pathways as newly acquired drivers in the metastatic clone.

SIGNATURE ANALYSIS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Mutational signature analysis of somatic point mutations identified dominant contributions from:
  - SBS3 (Homologous recombination deficiency; consistent with BRCA1 biallelic inactivation) вЂ” predominant
  - SBS2 / SBS13 (APOBEC cytidine deaminase activity) вЂ” secondary contribution
  - SBS1 (Age-related spontaneous deamination of 5-methylcytosine) вЂ” minor contribution

The predominance of SBS3 is consistent with the known germline BRCA1 loss-of-function and supports the clinical assessment of HRD-positive tumor biology.
